behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is at module handoff (HTTP handler -> signature verifier -> queue writer). No real AI/ML. Higher System was considered, but the behavior text only requires 401 + no forward and does not require real network/OS/subprocess; this can be validated with in-process HTTP wiring and a real queue adapter spy/readback.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real handler stack in-process, send a signed request with a tampered body, assert HTTP 401, and assert queue write count stays 0.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is necessary to catch verifier/handler/queue wiring bugs; unit-only checks can miss accidental enqueue paths.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an externally visible ordering side effect (write -> ack) requiring round-trip observation across boundary timing. Unit/integration fakes can hide ordering races. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Send a real HTTP request to a running service instance backed by the real persistent queue; capture response timepoint and verify the event is already durably readable from queue storage before/at 200 ack completion.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly exercises real boundary ordering, matching System.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is async orchestration between handler and downstream worker modules. No requirement for real OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond local app wiring; no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real handler + queue + a downstream processor stub that blocks (e.g., delayed promise). Send request and assert response returns immediately while processor completion occurs later.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is the lowest level that proves non-blocking module interaction.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a wall-clock SLA under concurrency; realistic scheduling/IO/runtime effects are the failure mode. Needs real runtime execution and end-to-end timing. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service process, create concurrent load with earlier slow downstream jobs, send a new webhook over real HTTP, and assert observed client round-trip latency is <3s.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Wall-clock concurrency behavior requires System-level timing observation.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires external side-effect readback (queue should not gain another record). Higher than unit due dedupe state handoff/persistence interaction. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the running webhook endpoint and verify via queue/store readback that only one durable entry exists.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System chosen due explicit round-trip side-effect visibility requirement.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit external error-path response contract (status + header). Integration was considered, but behavior text requires API contract visibility at boundary; no AI/ML involvement.
behaviors.test_description: Force real queue-full condition, call webhook over HTTP, and assert 503 plus correct Retry-After header in actual response.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System-level contract check matches the required boundary behavior.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Needs real queue implementation with persistence semantics across modules (not a mock), but does not inherently require real HTTP or subprocess boundaries. Agentic/Workflow not applicable.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue with real persistence backend in test environment, fill to configured capacity, then attempt one more enqueue and assert deterministic rejection/error code.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is sufficient because local real backend behavior is the target, without external service contract checks.
